behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local lint logic (string/template analysis). No real harness wiring, OS, subprocess, or model call is required. MOCK-MISS is low for this specific behavior because the leakage rule itself can be validated directly at function/object scope.
behaviors.test_description: Call the lint function with representative prompt-template strings: one clean template and multiple leaking templates containing phase instructions copied from SKILL.md sections. Assert reject/accept decisions and precise violation locations.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation/fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: The bug can appear only after module handoff (template + interpolation + fixtures + assembler). Unit tests of the template alone can miss leakage introduced during assembly. No real external infra or LLM dependency is required.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt-building pipeline with production-like fixtures/interpolation inputs, capture the assembled prompt, and run leakage detection on that final artifact. Include a fixture case known to have caused leakage to ensure the integration test would fail if assembly reintroduces phase text.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from the plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is wiring between invocation code and plugin/skill file loading. A unit test of a validator function would not prove context source is actually file-backed. Local filesystem use fits Integration per level defs.
behaviors.test_description: Execute test invocation against a temporary plugin/skill directory containing SKILL.md content, then assert the runtime context source is the loaded file content (not embedded constants) by changing file content and observing corresponding behavior expectation changes.

behaviors.description: If the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails with the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is explicitly about model behavior depending on skill context quality. Correctness depends on real LLM output sensitivity to context changes; mocked/deterministic substitutes can hide the regression. It is a single agentic step, not a multi-step workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Run the same user-style prompt twice against the real model through the harness: once with intact SKILL.md and once with the targeted section removed/degraded. Assert pass on intact context and fail on degraded context using the same behavioral criteria.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: Ordering bug appears at module boundaries (model call result handling -> artifact writer -> parser). A pure unit of parser logic cannot prove pre-parse capture happens in real flow. No real LLM is strictly required if malformed output is injected.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the response-processing pipeline with intentionally malformed model text. Assert a raw-output artifact is written with full content even when downstream parsing fails, and verify timestamp/order indicates artifact creation precedes parse/extract stage.

behaviors.description: Assertions verify structured behavior (for example field placement or schema shape), not mere keyword presence that could come from prompt echoing.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is assertion design/logic. You can catch false positives with crafted outputs (keyword echo vs correct structure) without full harness or model calls. Escalation is unnecessary unless validating parser integration specifically.
behaviors.test_description: Test assertion helpers with synthetic outputs: (a) prompt-echo text containing expected keywords but wrong structure, (b) correctly structured payload. Assert (a) fails and (b) passes based on schema/field placement checks rather than substring matching.
